prior.content_md:
**1. LCA Data Collection and Management Infrastructure**

BREEAM V7 mandates multi-stage Life Cycle Assessments (LCA) at concept, technical, and post-construction stages for Outstanding ratings, and at a minimum of one stage for Excellent. The workspace (as client/developer) must establish or commission a data management workflow that: (a) captures EPD (Environmental Product Declaration) links and dataset identifiers for all major material categories from concept stage; (b) ensures consistency of EN 15804+A2-aligned datasets across all three assessment stages — mixing A1-era and A2-era datasets across stages can invalidate the improvement narrative and fail QA; (c) explicitly includes MEP assemblies and refrigerant data within the LCA boundary, as these are now mandatory scope items; and (d) produces BREEAM Platform-ready reports well before QA submission deadlines. Gap from baseline: V6 LCA obligations were less prescriptive and could be discharged at technical design stage; V7 requires concept-stage initiation, representing a significant workflow shift for project teams that previously deferred carbon modelling.

**2. Energy Modelling — Restructured Ene 01–08 Framework**

The energy assessment category has been completely restructured into eight distinct issues (Ene 01–Ene 08). The workspace must ensure that any facility design brief and energy consultant appointment explicitly covers: predictive operational energy modelling (Ene 02) with options for third-party verified models; passive design strategy credits (Ene 04) addressed at building form stage; and demand-side response capability for all fixed building systems (Ene 07). For Outstanding targets, the brief must prohibit on-site fossil fuel combustion in the building services specification. Zero ozone depletion potential refrigerants must be specified across all HVAC and cold-store systems. Updated benchmarks are aligned with grid decarbonisation trajectories and differ materially from V6 benchmarks.

**3. Biodiversity and Ecological Assessment**

V7 requires ecological assessments aligned with ISO 18400 (International) and UK biodiversity legislation (UK). Biodiversity Net Gain credits can be achieved via DEFRA Biodiversity Metric or the Urban Greening Factor. For logistics, humanitarian pre-positioning, and event infrastructure facilities — which often occupy brownfield, peri-urban, or airport-adjacent land — an ecological baseline survey must be commissioned at site selection stage. Evidence of BNG must be traceable through handover documentation. Gap from baseline: biodiversity was a lower-weighted discretionary credit area in V6; it is now a core outcome with mandatory minimum thresholds for higher ratings.

**4. Indoor Air Quality Plan**

An IAQ Plan is a mandatory minimum standard for any project targeting Very Good or above. For workspace facilities handling fine art, film and TV equipment, luxury goods, and humanitarian medical cargo — where indoor environmental quality has direct implications for cargo integrity as well as occupant health — this plan must address product emission standards, ventilation design, and monitoring. The IAQ Plan must be commissioned at design stage and submitted as formal evidence.

**5. Training and Supplier / Contractor Onboarding**

Design and construction supply chain partners (architects, MEP engineers, structural engineers, main contractors) must be briefed on V7 requirements from appointment. V7 requires clearer proof of performance and deeper documentation than V6; contractors unfamiliar with V7 evidence standards are a material QA risk. The workspace should include V7 compliance obligations in design appointments and contractor pre-qualification criteria.

**6. ESG Reporting Integration**

V7 streamlines alignment with external reporting frameworks including EU Taxonomy, GRESB, and RICS WLCA. Workspaces managing assets for institutional investors or operating under SFDR or TCFD obligations should configure their internal sustainability data management systems to ingest BREEAM V7 assessment outputs directly. The optional EU Taxonomy evidence pathway activated at registration generates additional reporting outputs that can be used to support green bond or green loan documentation.

**7. New BREEAM Platform Transition**

V7 registrations and assessments are managed exclusively on the new BREEAM Platform (platform.breeam.com), not on the legacy BREEAM Projects portal. Third-party software integrations were not available at V7 launch on 30 September 2025; partners were advised to confirm their own readiness timelines. Workspaces using third-party project management or sustainability software that previously integrated with BREEAM Projects must verify whether their platforms have been updated to interface with the new BREEAM Platform.

*Source: BREEAM New Construction V7 FAQs, BRE Global, 2025–2026. https://breeam.com/breeam-newconstructionv7/breeam-version-7-faqs; One Click LCA — BREEAM V7 Compliance Guide, 2026. https://oneclicklca.com/en-gb/resources/articles/breeam-version-7-a-guide-for-compliance; Encon Associates — Key Changes BREEAM V6 and V7, October 2025. https://www.enconassociates.com/blog-insights/key-changes-breeam-v6-breeam-v7/; Stroma — Everything You Need to Know About BREEAM V7, November 2025. https://stroma.com/latest-news/everything-you-need-to-know-about-breeam-v7/*
